q: What does a support worker do with a receipt after a 10pm shift?
a: Shoves it in their pocket, puts their uniform in the wash, receipt is gone. They don't think about it again.

q: Why does finance chase receipts all month-end?
a: They can't code or close a transaction without a receipt. Multiply by 50 staff — that's their entire job. Not analysis — chasing paper.

q: Best opening question for Missing Receipts?
a: When a receipt doesn't come in, whose job is it to chase it? And what happens if they never get it?

q: What happens when a support worker sees 200 GL cost centres?
a: They pick the closest-looking one, always use the same one, or leave it blank. It will be wrong. Every time.

q: Why is wrong GL coding a compliance risk in NDIS?
a: A participant's budget gets charged for things it shouldn't. That's not an admin problem — that's a funding compliance problem.

q: Why will training never fix wrong coding?
a: It's a system design problem, not a behaviour problem. You're asking a carer to make an accounting decision at the end of a long shift. That will always fail.

q: A $340 hardware charge appears on a shared card. What can finance prove?
a: Nothing. They can't identify which of 4 staff used it, what it was for, or who authorised it. An auditor could destroy them.

q: What question instantly exposes the shared card problem?
a: When you look at a transaction on your card statement, can you tell me which staff member made that purchase?

q: What makes shared cards uniquely unrecoverable?
a: The information to verify who spent what was never captured. You can't reconstruct what never existed.

q: Are approvals a pre-spend or post-spend control?
a: Post-spend. Always. The money left the account the moment the card was tapped. Approvals happen after the fact.

q: What are the two approval scenarios and how do they differ?
a: Card spend: money already gone, approval is audit trail only — can't reverse the spend. Reimbursement: employee paid their own money, decline it and the company's money never moves. Real teeth.

q: What is the one question that exposes a fake approval process?
a: When your approver clicks approve — what are they actually able to verify in that moment? Almost nobody can answer this well.

q: What happens to the approval queue when the GM goes on leave?
a: It stops. 200 transactions pile up. They either rubber-stamp everything when they return (pointless) or review properly (takes days, month-end is tomorrow).

q: When does month-end pain typically resolve?
a: 14th-15th of the following month. Halfway through November before they know what happened in October.

q: What is the ONE root cause connecting all 5 pains?
a: The people spending money are not finance people. Every system relies on them doing the right thing. They won't — not because they're bad, because it's not their job.
